summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Unit Circle & Trigonometric Ratios
summary_sections.content_html:
Unit Circle: Circle with radius 1, center at origin. Point on circle: (cos ГЋВё, sin ГЋВё). Angles measured counterclockwise from (1, 0). Complete circle = 2ГЏв‚¬ radians. Special angles: 0, ГЏв‚¬/6, ГЏв‚¬/4, ГЏв‚¬/3, ГЏв‚¬/2, etc.

Trigonometric Ratios: sin ГЋВё = y, cos ГЋВё = x, tan ГЋВё = y/x (on unit circle). Also cot ГЋВё = x/y, sec ГЋВё = 1/x, csc ГЋВё = 1/y. SOHCAHTOA helps remember right triangle ratios.

Exact Values: Common angles have known exact values. sin(0) = 0, sin(ГЏв‚¬/6) = 1/2, sin(ГЏв‚¬/4) = ГўЛ†ЕЎ2/2, sin(ГЏв‚¬/3) = ГўЛ†ЕЎ3/2, sin(ГЏв‚¬/2) = 1. Cosine values reverse for complement.

Periodicity & Signs: Trigonometric functions repeat every 2ГЏв‚¬ radians. Signs vary by quadrant: Q1 all positive, Q2 sin positive, Q3 tan positive, Q4 cos positive.
